edges.description: Toxic or physical irritants injure the epidermis and precipitate barrier failure.
Barrier failure activates keratinocytes and promotes local proinflammatory signaling.
edges.target: Keratinocyte activation and proinflammatory mediator release

edges.description: Keratinocyte-derived inflammatory mediators recruit and activate additional inflammatory cells in skin.
edges.source: Keratinocyte activation and proinflammatory mediator release

edges.description: Inflammatory cell recruitment culminates in the clinically visible dermatitis lesion.
Hapten exposure activates innate cutaneous immune pathways during the sensitization phase.
edges.target: Skin innate immune activation during sensitization

edges.description: Innate activation in skin supports dendritic-cell-mediated priming of allergen-specific T cells.
edges.source: Skin innate immune activation during sensitization

edges.description: Loss of regulatory control permits priming to haptens in susceptible individuals.
edges.source: Breakdown of regulatory T-cell-mediated hapten tolerance

edges.description: Loss of suppressive T-cell control permits persistence and amplification of eczema in allergic patients.
edges.source: Breakdown of regulatory T-cell-mediated hapten tolerance

edges.description: Sensitized allergen-specific T cells are recruited back to skin after hapten re-exposure.
Re-exposure activates epidermal resident memory T cells and intensifies the flare response.
Activated resident memory T cells drive a broader inflammatory cytokine and chemokine program.
edges.target: Mixed type 1 and type 3 cytokine and chemokine amplification

edges.description: Type 1 lymphocyte-derived IFNg reprograms dermal fibroblasts into chemokine-producing inflammatory stromal cells.
edges.source: Mixed type 1 and type 3 cytokine and chemokine amplification

edges.description: Fibroblast chemokine reinforcement sustains the inflammatory dermatitis lesion in affected skin.
Sensory neuron activation contributes to the itching phenotype in allergic contact dermatitis.

nodes.description: During sensitization, antigen-presenting cells prime allergen-specific T cells, establishing the adaptive immune memory required for later flares.

biological_processes: dendritic cell antigen processing and presentation

evidence.reference_title: Effector and regulatory mechanisms in allergic contact dermatitis.
evidence.snippet: Allergic contact dermatitis, also referred to as contact hypersensitivity, is mediated by CD8+ T cells, which are primed in lymphoid organs during the sensitization phase and are recruited in the skin upon re-exposure to the hapten.

nodes.description: Allergic contact dermatitis reflects failure of suppressive CD4-positive regulatory T-cell programs that normally prevent hapten priming and dampen eczema in sensitized individuals.
nodes.id: Breakdown of regulatory T-cell-mediated hapten tolerance

evidence.reference_title: Effector and regulatory mechanisms in allergic contact dermatitis.
evidence.snippet: Subsets of CD4+ T cells endowed with suppressive activity are responsible for both the down-regulation of eczema in allergic patients and the prevention of priming to haptens in nonallergic individuals.

nodes.description: In allergic contact dermatitis, complement-associated neuroimmune signaling activates peripheral sensory pathways and contributes to itch generation.

evidence.reference_title: Proteome Profile of Trigeminal Ganglion in Murine Model of Allergic Contact Dermatitis: Complement 3 Pathway Contributes to Itch and Pain Sensation.
evidence.snippet: The complement system in sensory ganglion might play an essential role in forming pruritic and nociceptive sensations in ACD.

evidence.reference_title: Identifying the causes of contact dermatitis.
evidence.snippet: The two most common variants are irritant contact dermatitis (ICD) and allergic contact dermatitis (ACD).

nodes.description: Irritant injury disrupts epidermal barrier integrity and creates the tissue context for downstream inflammatory activation.
Dermal fibroblasts adopt a chemokine-producing inflammatory phenotype that reinforces type 1 lymphocyte polarization and sustains cutaneous inflammation.

evidence.reference_title: Defining cell type-specific immune responses in a mouse model of allergic contact dermatitis by single-cell transcriptomics.
evidence.snippet: IFNγ treatment shifted dermal fibroblasts from collagen-producing to CXCL9/10-producing, which promoted T cell polarization toward the type-1 phenotype through a CXCR3-dependent mechanism.

nodes.description: In allergic contact dermatitis, repeated skin contact with small reactive chemicals exposes the immune system to haptens and initiates sensitization.

evidence.reference_title: Effector and regulatory mechanisms in allergic contact dermatitis.
evidence.snippet: Allergic contact dermatitis (ACD), one of the commonest occupational diseases, is a T-cell-mediated skin inflammation caused by repeated skin exposure to contact allergens, i.e. nonprotein chemicals called haptens.

nodes.description: Irritant-induced mediators recruit and activate inflammatory cells in skin after barrier injury.

evidence.snippet: release of proinflammatory mediators that directly recruit and activate T lymphocytes.

nodes.description: In irritant contact dermatitis, direct exposure to chemical or physical irritants causes the initiating toxic cutaneous insult.

evidence.snippet: It represents the cutaneous response to the toxic/physical effects of a wide variety of environmental agents.

nodes.description: Barrier-injured keratinocytes enter an activated inflammatory state and release mediators that propagate local cutaneous inflammation.
nodes.id: Keratinocyte activation and proinflammatory mediator release

evidence.snippet: involves skin barrier disruption, cellular changes, and release of proinflammatory mediators.

Irritant contact dermatitis is a result of activated innate immune response to various external stimuli and consists of complex interplay which involves skin barrier disruption, cellular changes, and release of proinflammatory mediators.

nodes.description: Activated effector and resident memory T cells amplify IFNg-, IL-17A-, IL-1beta-, and chemokine-rich inflammatory programs, with predominant type 1 and type 3 effector responses and lesser type 2 contributions.
nodes.id: Mixed type 1 and type 3 cytokine and chemokine amplification

evidence.reference_title: CD100 boosts the inflammatory response in the challenge phase of allergic contact dermatitis in mice.
evidence.snippet: We show that loss of CD100 results in a reduced inflammatory response to DNFB with impaired production of IFNγ, IL-17A, CXCL1, CXCL2, CXCL5, and IL-1β and decreased recruitment of neutrophils to the epidermis.
All allergens exhibited mixed effector immune responses, mainly type 1 and 3 immunity, and, to a lesser extent, type 2 immunity.

evidence.reference_title: Eyelid Contact Dermatitis: 25-Year Single-Center Retrospective Study.
evidence.snippet: Background/Objectives: Eyelid dermatitis is an inflammatory disease affecting the palpebral skin characterized by itching, edema, and scaling of the periorbital area.

nodes.description: Upon renewed hapten exposure, primed effector T cells re-enter skin and initiate the elicitation phase of allergic contact dermatitis.

evidence.reference_title: Effector and regulatory mechanisms in allergic contact dermatitis.
evidence.snippet: Allergic contact dermatitis, also referred to as contact hypersensitivity, is mediated by CD8+ T cells, which are primed in lymphoid organs during the sensitization phase and are recruited in the skin upon re-exposure to the hapten.

nodes.description: Epidermal resident memory CD8-positive T cells are activated in allergen-experienced skin and intensify local flare responses.

evidence.reference_title: CD100 boosts the inflammatory response in the challenge phase of allergic contact dermatitis in mice.
evidence.snippet: BACKGROUND: Allergic contact dermatitis (ACD) is an inflammatory disease with a complex pathophysiology in which epidermal-resident memory CD8+ T (TRM ) cells play a key role.

nodes.description: Early innate immune signaling in allergen-exposed skin shapes the sensitization phase and supports subsequent adaptive immune priming.
nodes.id: Skin innate immune activation during sensitization

evidence.reference_title: Effector and regulatory mechanisms in allergic contact dermatitis.
evidence.snippet: Recent advances in the pathophysiology of ACD have demonstrated the important role of skin innate immunity in the sensitization process
